gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A validated reconstruction manifest — the RFC-0003 §6 record: mode, model, dim, codebooks,"] # [doc = " optional recipe, decode procedure + params, and the `{ε,δ,strength}` bound certificate."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Construction goes through [`ReconManifest::new`] (validates at build time) or"] # [doc = " [`ReconManifest::validate`] (validates an already-built [`ReconInfo`] from the kernel). A"] # [doc = " well-formed `ReconManifest` is the only way to call `regrow`; an over-strength resonator"] # [doc = " manifest is unrepresentable as a `ReconManifest`."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub struct ReconManifest { inner : ReconInfo , }
gaps.reason: struct `ReconManifest` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl ReconManifest { # [doc = " Build and validate a reconstruction manifest from its components."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Delegates to [`ReconInfo::new`] for the kernel-level invariants (model non-empty, dim ≥ 1,"] # [doc = " codebooks non-empty, mode/recipe consistency, decode-procedure constraints, bound"] # [doc = " well-formedness), then applies the `std.spore` additional check:"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **FR-C2 ceiling**: if `decode.procedure == Resonator`, the bound basis must not exceed"] # [doc = "   `Empirical`. The kernel (`ReconInfo::new`) enforces this; a violation is caught as"] # [doc = "   `WfError::MalformedReconstruction` and mapped to `Err(MalformedManifest::KernelWf)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " Validation is a pure predicate; the same inputs always produce the same outcome."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(MalformedManifest::KernelWf)`"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All kernel-level well-formedness violations (bad mode/bound, missing recipe/decode param,"] # [doc = " FR-C2 resonator-ceiling exceeded) return `Err(KernelWf)` — the specific kernel rule"] # [doc = " violated is described in the `WfError` message (C1/G2). Use [`ReconManifest::validate`] on"] # [doc = " a deserialized `ReconInfo` to get the `ResonatorOverStrength` variant explicitly."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] pub fn new (mode : ReconMode , model : impl Into < String > , dim : u32 , codebooks : Vec < mycelium_core :: ContentHash > , recipe : Option < mycelium_core :: recon :: Recipe > , decode : mycelium_core :: recon :: DecodeSpec , bound : mycelium_core :: bound :: Bound ,) -> Result < Self , MalformedManifest > { let inner = ReconInfo :: new (mode , model , dim , codebooks , recipe , decode , bound) . map_err (| _ : WfError | MalformedManifest :: KernelWf) ? ; Ok (ReconManifest { inner }) } # [doc = " Validate an existing [`ReconInfo`] from the kernel, wrapping it as a [`ReconManifest`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the path used when a `ReconInfo` arrives via deserialization or is produced by the"] # [doc = " kernel's own construction. The kernel's invariants are already enforced; this layer surfaces"] # [doc = " them in the `std.spore` error vocabulary."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The resonator over-strength check is redundant here (the kernel enforces it), but is kept"] # [doc = " explicit to document the invariant at this layer (VR-5 / no black boxes)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `Err(MalformedManifest::ResonatorOverStrength)`"] # [doc = " If the manifest carries a `Resonator` decode whose bound basis exceeds `Empirical`."] # [doc = " In practice this should be unreachable (the kernel already refuses), but the check is"] # [doc = " present for defense-in-depth (C1/G2 — never silently trust a carry-in)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] pub fn validate (inner : ReconInfo) -> Result < Self , MalformedManifest > { if inner . decode () . procedure == mycelium_core :: recon :: DecodeProcedure :: Resonator && inner . bound () . basis . strength () . rank () < GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical . rank () { return Err (MalformedManifest :: ResonatorOverStrength) ; } Ok (ReconManifest { inner }) } # [doc = " The reconstruction mode (`IndexedRetrieval` or `CompositionalReconstruction`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn mode (& self) -> ReconMode { self . inner . mode () } # [doc = " The declared guarantee strength from the manifest's bound certificate."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For a `Resonator` decode this is always ≤ `Empirical` (enforced at construction)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn declared_strength (& self) -> GuaranteeStrength { self . inner . bound () . basis . strength () } # [doc = " The content hash of the manifest, computed by hashing its canonical representation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Uses the kernel's content-hash surface (M-103 / ADR-003): the same manifest always"] # [doc = " produces the same hash; metadata is not identity."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn manifest_hash (& self) -> mycelium_core :: ContentHash { let json = serde_json :: to_string (& self . inner) . expect ("ReconInfo is always serializable") ; let hex = blake3 :: hash (json . as_bytes ()) . to_hex () ; mycelium_core :: ContentHash :: from_parts ("blake3" , hex . as_str ()) . expect ("blake3 hex is a valid digest") } # [doc = " Access the inner [`ReconInfo`] for callers that need the kernel representation (e.g."] # [doc = " `std.vsa` reconstruct_* functions)."] # [must_use] pub fn inner (& self) -> & ReconInfo { & self . inner } # [doc = " The bound's failure-probability δ, if this is a `ProbabilityBound` (the common case for"] # [doc = " VSA resonator regrowth)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `None` for other bound kinds (e.g. `ErrorBound`, `CrosstalkBound`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (deterministic)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: `None` when the bound is not a probability bound"] # [doc = " # Effects: none"] # [must_use] pub fn delta (& self) -> Option < f64 > { match & self . inner . bound () . kind { mycelium_core :: bound :: BoundKind :: Probability { delta } => Some (* delta) , _ => None , } } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A refusal from manifest validation — explicitly named, never silent (C1/G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each variant names the violated invariant so callers can surface the specific rule"] # [doc = " violation (G11 dual projection — the error is both the refusal *and* the diagnostic)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum MalformedManifest { # [doc = " The manifest's decode is `Resonator` but its bound basis exceeds `Empirical` (FR-C2"] # [doc = " violation). A resonator decode is probabilistic-only and can never be `Proven`."] ResonatorOverStrength , # [doc = " A kernel-level well-formedness violation: bad mode/bound, missing recipe/decode param."] # [doc = " Covers `ReconInfo::new` refusals that do not correspond to the FR-C2 ceiling specifically."] KernelWf , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The result of a probabilistic regrowth attempt via `std.vsa`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Carries the `Factorization` returned by `std.vsa::reconstruct_factors` plus the"] # [doc = " `GuaranteeStrength` tag from the manifest's bound certificate — always ≤ `Empirical`"] # [doc = " for the resonator path (FR-C2 enforced at manifest construction time)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # `Approx<T>` coupling (FLAG Q4a — RESOLVED)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `RegrowthResult` carries the manifest's **full `{ε,δ,strength}` certificate bound** (with its"] # [doc = " `BoundBasis`), so it projects losslessly to the stdlib's honest carrier via"] # [doc = " [`RegrowthResult::as_approx`] — `std.numerics::Approx<Factorization>` — with **no fabricated"] # [doc = " basis**: the strength is *derived* from the bound's basis ([`Approx::attach`], VR-5 — never"] # [doc = " upgraded). It carries `Factorization` rather than a `core::Value` because the resonator decode"] # [doc = " yields VSA factor atoms, not a reconstructed `Value` (that mapping is `std.vsa`'s, not spore's)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct RegrowthResult { # [doc = " The recovered factor atoms from the resonator (or cleanup) decode."] pub factorization : Factorization , # [doc = " The manifest's `{ε,δ,strength}` certificate bound — its basis determines the honest"] # [doc = " strength. **Private (the FR-C2 seal):** a public field would let a caller hand-build a"] # [doc = " `RegrowthResult` with a `Proven` basis and project it to a `Proven` `Approx`, exceeding the"] # [doc = " probabilistic-regrowth ceiling. Construction goes through [`RegrowthResult::new`], which"] # [doc = " refuses an over-strength basis; read it via [`RegrowthResult::bound`]."] bound : Bound , }
gaps.reason: struct `RegrowthResult` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl RegrowthResult { # [doc = " Construct a regrowth result, **refusing** a bound whose basis is stronger than `Empirical`"] # [doc = " (the FR-C2 / VR-5 probabilistic-regrowth ceiling) with an explicit"] # [doc = " [`MalformedManifest::ResonatorOverStrength`] — never a silent accept. This makes"] # [doc = " [`as_approx`](Self::as_approx) structurally incapable of producing a strength above"] # [doc = " `Empirical`: the ceiling holds by construction, not by comment."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`MalformedManifest::ResonatorOverStrength`] if `bound`'s basis implies a strength"] # [doc = " stronger than `Empirical` (i.e. `Exact` or `Proven`)."] pub fn new (factorization : Factorization , bound : Bound) -> Result < Self , MalformedManifest > { if bound . basis . strength () . rank () < GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical . rank () { return Err (MalformedManifest :: ResonatorOverStrength) ; } Ok (Self { factorization , bound , }) } # [doc = " The certificate bound (read-only — construction enforces the FR-C2 ceiling)."] # [must_use] pub fn bound (& self) -> & Bound { & self . bound } # [doc = " The honest guarantee strength — **derived** from the bound's basis (never fabricated,"] # [doc = " never upgraded; VR-5). Always ≤ `Empirical` for a resonator decode (FR-C2, enforced by"] # [doc = " [`new`](Self::new))."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` (this is a pure predicate)"] # [doc = " # Fallibility: total"] # [must_use] pub fn strength (& self) -> GuaranteeStrength { self . bound . basis . strength () } # [doc = " The failure-probability δ from the certificate, when the bound is a `ProbabilityBound`"] # [doc = " (the common case for the resonator path); `None` for other bound kinds."] # [must_use] pub fn delta (& self) -> Option < f64 > { match & self . bound . kind { mycelium_core :: bound :: BoundKind :: Probability { delta } => Some (* delta) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Project to the stdlib's honest carrier `std.numerics::Approx<Factorization>` (FLAG Q4a —"] # [doc = " RESOLVED). The strength is derived from the bound's basis via [`Approx::attach`] — never"] # [doc = " upgraded (VR-5); the δ rides along inside the carried `bound`."] # [must_use] pub fn as_approx (& self) -> Approx < Factorization > { Approx :: attach (self . factorization . clone () , self . bound . clone ()) } # [doc = " True iff the strength is exactly `Empirical` (the expected case for the resonator path)."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empirical (& self) -> bool { self . strength () == GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical } # [doc = " True iff the strength is `Declared` (the weakest; user-asserted only)."] # [must_use] pub fn is_declared (& self) -> bool { self . strength () == GuaranteeStrength :: Declared } }

gaps.snippet:
# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use mycelium_core :: { bound :: { Bound , BoundBasis , BoundKind } , content :: operation_hash , recon :: { DecodeProcedure , DecodeSpec } , } ; fn empirical_bound () -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : 0.05 } , basis : BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials : 1000 , method : "test" . to_owned () , } , } } fn proven_bound () -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps : 0.01 , norm : mycelium_core :: bound :: NormKind :: L2 , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : "test theorem" . to_owned () , } , } } fn declared_bound () -> Bound { Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : 0.1 } , basis : BoundBasis :: UserDeclared , } } fn cleanup_decode () -> DecodeSpec { DecodeSpec { procedure : DecodeProcedure :: Cleanup , cleanup_threshold : Some (0.3) , factors : None , iteration_budget : None , cleanup : None , beta : None , tau_lock : None , init : None , seed : None , } } fn resonator_decode () -> DecodeSpec { DecodeSpec { procedure : DecodeProcedure :: Resonator , cleanup_threshold : None , factors : Some (vec ! [operation_hash ("factor-a") , operation_hash ("factor-b")]) , iteration_budget : Some (50) , cleanup : None , beta : None , tau_lock : None , init : None , seed : None , } } # [doc = " An IndexedRetrieval + Cleanup + EmpiricalFit manifest is valid."] # [test] fn valid_indexed_cleanup_manifest_is_accepted () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) ; assert ! (m . is_ok () , "{m:?}") ; } # [doc = " A Resonator + EmpiricalFit manifest is valid (the expected canonical case)."] # [test] fn valid_resonator_empirical_manifest_is_accepted () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , resonator_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) ; assert ! (m . is_ok () , "{m:?}") ; } # [doc = " A Resonator + Declared manifest is valid (Declared is weaker than Empirical — OK)."] # [doc = " Guard: rejecting Declared-basis resonator manifests violates the spec (only ProvenThm is"] # [doc = " forbidden — the rule is \"must not exceed Empirical\", not \"must be exactly Empirical\")."] # [test] fn resonator_declared_basis_is_accepted () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , resonator_decode () , declared_bound () ,) ; assert ! (m . is_ok () , "Declared is weaker than Empirical and must be accepted: {m:?}") ; } # [doc = " A Resonator + ProvenThm manifest via `new()` is REFUSED (FR-C2 ceiling violated)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The kernel (`ReconInfo::new`) catches this as `WfError::MalformedReconstruction`, which"] # [doc = " maps to `MalformedManifest::KernelWf` here. The `ResonatorOverStrength` variant is the"] # [doc = " defense-in-depth path via `validate()` for deserialized carry-ins."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guard: accepting this (returning Ok) violates FR-C2 — a probabilistic resonator decode"] # [doc = " can never be Proven."] # [test] fn resonator_proven_basis_is_refused_via_new () { let err = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , resonator_decode () , proven_bound () ,) . unwrap_err () ; assert_eq ! (err , MalformedManifest :: KernelWf , "new() must map the kernel's FR-C2 refusal to KernelWf (not ResonatorOverStrength)") ; } # [doc = " The `validate` path also refuses a (hypothetically constructed) over-strength manifest."] # [doc = " This tests the defense-in-depth re-check in `validate`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " NOTE: Since `ReconInfo::new` already enforces this, we cannot create a real"] # [doc = " over-strength `ReconInfo` directly. Instead we test `validate` with a valid manifest"] # [doc = " and verify `declared_strength()` is never above `Empirical` for the resonator path."] # [test] fn validate_resonator_manifest_strength_is_at_most_empirical () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , resonator_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; let strength = m . declared_strength () ; assert ! (strength . rank () >= GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical . rank () , "resonator manifest strength must not exceed Empirical (rank must be ≥ 2); \
             got {:?} (rank {})" , strength , strength . rank ()) ; } # [doc = " `mode()` and `declared_strength()` are deterministic (Exact)."] # [test] fn mode_and_strength_are_deterministic () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (m . mode () , ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval) ; assert_eq ! (m . declared_strength () , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical) ; } # [doc = " `manifest_hash()` is deterministic — same manifest always produces the same hash."] # [doc = " Guard: randomness in manifest_hash makes this fail."] # [test] fn manifest_hash_is_deterministic () { let m1 = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; let m2 = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (m1 . manifest_hash () , m2 . manifest_hash () , "manifest_hash must be deterministic (Exact)") ; assert ! (m1 . manifest_hash () . as_str () . starts_with ("blake3:") , "manifest_hash must use blake3 algorithm") ; } # [doc = " Different manifests produce different hashes."] # [doc = " Guard: returning a constant hash from manifest_hash makes this fail."] # [test] fn different_manifests_produce_different_hashes () { let m1 = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook-a")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; let m2 = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 2048 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook-a")] , None , cleanup_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; assert_ne ! (m1 . manifest_hash () , m2 . manifest_hash () , "manifests with different dims must hash differently") ; } # [doc = " `delta()` returns the probability bound's δ when present."] # [test] fn delta_returns_probability_bound_delta () { let m = ReconManifest :: new (ReconMode :: IndexedRetrieval , "MAP-I" , 1024 , vec ! [operation_hash ("codebook")] , None , resonator_decode () , empirical_bound () ,) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (m . delta () , Some (0.05)) ; } # [doc = " Error message for ResonatorOverStrength names the violated rule (G11 dual projection)."] # [test] fn resonator_over_strength_error_message_names_fr_c2 () { let msg = format ! ("{}" , MalformedManifest :: ResonatorOverStrength) ; assert ! (msg . contains ("Resonator") || msg . contains ("resonator") , "error must mention Resonator: {msg}") ; assert ! (msg . contains ("Empirical") , "error must mention Empirical ceiling: {msg}") ; } # [doc = " `RegrowthResult::is_empirical()` matches the Empirical strength."] # [test] fn regrowth_result_strength_predicates () { use mycelium_vsa :: { Factorization , ResonatorTrace , StopReason } ; let trace = ResonatorTrace { stop : StopReason :: Converged , iterations : 3 , trajectory : vec ! [] , final_decode : vec ! [] , } ; let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : 0.05 } , basis : BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { trials : 1000 , method : "resonator decode" . into () , } , } ; let r = RegrowthResult :: new (Factorization { factors : vec ! [] , trace , } , bound ,) . expect ("Empirical basis is within the FR-C2 ceiling") ; assert ! (r . is_empirical ()) ; assert ! (! r . is_declared ()) ; assert_eq ! (r . strength () , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical) ; assert_eq ! (r . delta () , Some (0.05)) ; let approx = r . as_approx () ; assert_eq ! (approx . strength () , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical) ; assert_eq ! (& approx . bound , r . bound ()) ; } # [doc = " FR-C2 seal: `RegrowthResult::new` **refuses** a bound whose basis is stronger than"] # [doc = " `Empirical` (a `Proven`/`Exact` regrowth is dishonest for the probabilistic resonator path)"] # [doc = " — an explicit `ResonatorOverStrength`, never a silent accept. This is what makes"] # [doc = " `as_approx()` structurally unable to exceed the ceiling."] # [test] fn regrowth_result_refuses_over_strength_basis () { use mycelium_vsa :: { Factorization , ResonatorTrace , StopReason } ; let proven = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Probability { delta : 0.0 } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : "bogus — regrowth is never proven" . into () , } , } ; let trace = ResonatorTrace { stop : StopReason :: Converged , iterations : 1 , trajectory : vec ! [] , final_decode : vec ! [] , } ; let r = RegrowthResult :: new (Factorization { factors : vec ! [] , trace , } , proven ,) ; assert_eq ! (r . unwrap_err () , MalformedManifest :: ResonatorOverStrength) ; } }
